transcript: And, uh, none other than Mr. Tom Landis, Esquire… gonna coordinate… a call with the man himself.
A thunderin’ applause! A thunderin’ applause!
Okay! Excellent, excellent, excellent! [delay, reverb]So, uh, let’s have a little fun this evening.—
[quiet] Let’s get some academics outta the way!
[laughs] I hope you can hear me okay!—
How much—? Can you hear him okay?
Woo! Woo! [delay, reverb]One hell of a deal! Chattanooga, you’ve got yourself one hell of a deal up 'ere! [exhales] [delay, reverb]Thank you.—
So tell me, how much do you plan out or improvise?
Uh… mostly improvise, very little planned out. Yeah!—
Then maybe the occasional bullet point or… text message to myself, but, uh, mostly just, uh—
Your call has been forwarded to an automated voice messaging system.—
That’s— Yeah. Just occurred. Pretty much, yeah.
Have you engaged in any other forms of pranks?
[laughs] Yes. Uh… Ya know… No. I’m gonna just do a 180 on that.

I just stick to the phone. I have some fun with some different things, ya know, here and there, but ya know! [dps]It’s just here ’n there and everything. [delay, pitch-shifted]Mostly the phone. I’m a phone type o’ guy. Sometimes I go to a payphone and, uh… just prank myself by not knowing what I’m doing there. But, uh… No, that’s just, uh… the exception, not the rule. Not the rule at all! [inhales]
Well let’s take some questions! Folks, what’s up?
Do you have a day job?
Do you have a day job?
I do. I drive. I drive and I deliver and I shop for people. And occasionally I record bands. That’s pretty much my two things that I do.
I’m happy to talk. Answer any questions you might have. Call any numbers you might have. Anything o’ that nature.
Hey, can I talk to Emilio real quick, please?
I just need to talk to him real quick. Sound good or…?
Yes! Seriously, is Emilio Senior there, please? It’s Peter.
Can I have your phone number?
Uh… no, I’m just gonna need to talk to him, okay? I don’t really have a lotta time or else I’d… love to play with ya, so… [inhales]
Can you put 'im on or not?
N— I don’t believe that, so…
I don’t believe you! Whadda you think of that?
Mm, well I don’t really care about your opinion, 'cause he’s not here.
Oh, is that a matter of fact? Well…
[ Wayne Bent sample ]One day, by myself, I sat down in my chair just relaxing and then God said to me "You are Messiah."
Yeah, you’re one stupid mother f-er.
You wanna… go out and go dancin’, get some nachos? Whadda ya think?
[ Wayne Bent sample ] [delay]And having sex was God’s instructions.
[ Wayne Bent sample ]Do what God tells you!
I’m gonna report you to the police.
[ Wayne Bent sample ]An MD is far—
[ Wayne Bent sample ]An MD is far more likely—
No, no, no. Don’t give a damn. Don’t give a damn, I’m reporting you.—
[ Wayne Bent samples ]An MD—An MD is far more likely to think of sex than I am.
[ Wayne Bent samples ]MDs even put their fingers in women’s private parts… or whatever else they do.Uh… MDs are with naked people.
Just put 'im on the phone and quit lollygaggin'.—
Alright, bye. I’m repo—reporting you to the police.
Put 'im on the phone, quit lollygagging!
Chattanooga, how are you toniiight?! Woo! [delay, reverb, pitch-shifted]
We are back on course here and we are movin’ along. Movin’ along real well here tonight. 'Kay.
I got a flexible straw—like a big long one—like 22 inches.
And I’m gonna bring it in there— just dip it in some different bottles, takes some tastes o’ some different things, kinda figure out which direction I wanna go tonight, alright?
Oh, uh… I’m not really sure what you’re talkin’ about, there. [laughs]
Oh, I’m just gonna bring my straw up there and just dip it in some different bottles, different cans, different glasses…
Just see whatcha got on tap—
Oh, uh… this is, uh… Golbriath.
Alright, Brian, I’m gonna be lookin’ out for you and your big ass straw.
And if you bring it in here, I’m gonna steal it from you! Uh… just be warned. [laughs]
Don’t do that! Don’t do that at all.—
[laughs] Welp, thank you, ah… If you’ve got a number, please feel free to share it. I’ll get up there and get involved with ya— mix things up a bit here and a bit there.
I sent you that one earlier.
Yeah, I’m goin’ through 'em. I’m goin' through 'em.
So we have some more Chattanoogan fun lined up—another one. It is ringing.’Ello? Barry? Hey, Barry!
I’m doin’ fine! How’re ya doin’, Duncan?

Pretty decent, pretty decent! I just… moved into the neighborhood here and I had been tryin’ to reach ya all week, really, but I couldn’t get through. Hey, um… I gotta go back outta town… for about two weeks and I just went ahead and, uh… forwarded my mail to your address. Just for two weeks. I tried to get ahold o’ ya beforehand but I couldn’t get through, doggone it! So… Just a heads-up there, Barry, okay?—
Yes, sir. Yes, sir. Not a big deal. Just for about two—
No, you can’t do that, man!
Well I’ve already done it, so… I didn’t know who else to—
But I— Well, I mean… why were you doin’ that?

I—I’m new to the area, ya know, like I say. It’s just for three weeks. If you could go 'head and… just keep everything alphabetized—that kinda stuff—when I come back, that’d be real handy for me, ya know?
I appreciate ye! I—I appreciate ya!—
No, I’m not gon’ do that. [laughs]
[laughing] N— No, no. No, I can’t do that, no.—
Listen, Barry, I got your number from, uh… from Old Ironsides up at the fillin’ station up 'ere.
Who in the hell are you talkin’ about? [laughs]
Old Ironsides gave me your number, guy. He works at Reuters. Guy knows everybody! And listen—
Man, I don’t— I don’t know— I don’t know who you are. [laughs]
Ya know, if you see anything real urgent, Barry—anything real urgent, real pressing—just go 'head and, um…—
[laughs] Man, you’re— Man, you’re nuts, man! No, man. [unclear]
Okay. Goodbye, Barry. Thank you anyway.
[sample of Wayne Bent disciples cheering, shouting "Liberty!"]
Chattanooga![delay] Woo! Woo, Chattanooga, woo!So… not the kinda thing to spring on somebody, is what I’m hearing. Uh…
Hey, there. Can I request, a… a number? Can I put a number in? I got one.—
Sure! Yeah, yeah! Whadda ya got?
I got a— It’s Steven. The phone number is 7-1-2—[redacted].
[sample of Wayne Bent disciple]This means literally—

I— Yeah, Kip. Kippy. ’St moved in 'round the corner from ya here. I’ve been tryin' to call ya all week but I couldn’t get through, um… [motorboats]Look, I just, uh… gotta go outta town again for a couple weeks and I… went ahead and had my mail forwarded over to your… address. Just for two, three weeks, while I’m outta town. I just wanted to give you a li’l heads-up and notify you… here tonight, okay?
Well I’m— I’m glad you, uh, you notified me. Uh, uh… you can definitely have your stuff mailed to my place.
For the next two, three weeks, while you’re outta town.
Thank you! Yeah, I’ll be in, uh… Canada… for that time.
Yeah, exactly. It’ll probably feel like—
…more like three months but… [laughs] No, it’s just gonna be three weeks.—
Do you feel guided to go there?
No… No. It’s for a job interview.—
Did The Spirit—? Did The Spirit call?
Yeah, it’s a job interview, ya know?
So… Uh, hey, if you see anything real pressing in there, please just go 'head and pay it. I’ll certainly reimburse you when I get back.
It’s— It’s already happened. There was a check for two hu— There was a bill for two-hundred forty-two bucks.—

Spirit Airlines. It said "Spirit calls" on it. "Spirit calls you to Vancouver". Paid for that flight. Um, I also sent a good recommendation into your… to your job interview. So you should— You should just say that Not two, three weeks—two years you’ll be stayin’ there. So you should, actually, just send me the mail for a good…
I’ll—I’ll have to— just have to foot the bill for a good few years.
So there you are. Yeah, okay.
Oh, great! Okay. Perfect. Thank you!
Hey, this is Greggy. Uh… I needed to set a time with you… to come out and, uh… get some specs to install your alarm system. I’m with McNeil Security.
I’m s’posed to install your security system for you. So, um… when’s a good time to come out? Uh… maybe Mondee or…? Maybe Mondee, Tuesdee?—
Um, I don’t set that up 'cause I’m just in the dispatch. Um, so Heather would— That would be Heather.
Uh… and what’s the best time to reach Heather to install your security system? You got a brand new security system comin’ your way! That’s gotta feel pretty good, right?
Okay, so go 'head and meet with her on Monday at 8 AM, you’re saying?
Okay, I’ll be there bright ’n early—8 AM—on Monday. And—
Um, no, I just work third.
Yeah, I’m in the dispatch room.
Oh, yeah? D’you maybe wanna go dancin’ this evening or do something o’ that kind?
Please state your name after the tone and Google Voice will try to connect you.
"No, thanks"? Well, maybe you can think about it.—
We will call you back when we can. Thank you.
We got all weekend. Know what I’m sayin’?
We can try this, try that, maybe make a… mixed drink o’ some kind…
…make some… salza—homemade salsa! Somethin’ I like to have, from time to time. Does that sound good at all?
But who did you say you were again?
…is not available. At the tone, please record your message.—
…and gonna be installing your brand new security system… on Monday morning at 8 o’clock!—
When you are finished recording, you may hang up or press "1" for more options.—
The Google subscriber you have called is not available.
So it’s gonna be a lotta fun. You’re gonna love what ya see.—
Please leave a message after the tone. [beep]
You’re gonna love the way ya feel! 'Cause it’s a real secure feeling that you’re gonna have. Does that sounds like a winner?
Sure, but I don’t think, um…
Hello! You’ve reached the voice message mailbox for Bob Agra number two!—
I don’t think they do security systems at [unclear]. I think we do our own security system.
No, I was called to install one of ours for you guys… is what I was called to do.
Mm, well we— I think we do our own security system.
Uh, not this time. We’ve got— We’ve got what’s hot.—
Your call has been forwarded to an automated voice message system.
We’ve got a real hot product this summer.
You’re gonna love the way it makes ya feel!
You’re not gonna wanna leave work you’re gonna feel so good up there!—
At the tone, please record your message.—
Okay, well you can call back and talk to Heather Monday. Thank you.—
When you have finished recording, you may hang up or press "1" for more options.—
I’ll be there— I’ll be there Monday morning and we’ll just get this… get this booger installed!—
If you’re somebody I know, I’ll call you back. [beep]—
Alright, you’re gonna love it! Thank you for your time! You’re gonna love what happens. See— Oh! Sure ya are!—
[sample of children singing a voicemail parody of “Party In The U.S.A.” by Miley Cyrus]
At the tone, please record your voice message.
It’s gonna be wonderful! You’re gonna love everything that happens.
When you are finished recording, you may hang up or press "1" for more options [beep].
Lookin’ around like yeah!So leave a message after the tone!We’ll call you back anyway!Yeah, yeah, yeahyeah!Leave a message anyway!
You’re gonna b— Go about everything in a whole new way! You’re— Everything in a whole new way, Chattanooga! Chattanooga, thank you so much! Chattanooga, thank you so much!

Thank you so much! Chattanooga, thank you!
[raps in early hip hop flow]I’m glad you called but I’m not home!But I’ll be back before too long!You gotta leave your name, you gotta leave your number!Wait! For! The beep![beep]—
Goodnight! Chattanooga, Chattanooga, thank you, thank you, thank you!
